quizQuestion:
De niño, {___} mucho con mis amigos.
(As a child, I talked a lot with my friends.)
sentenceTranslation: As a child, I talked a lot with my friends.
explanation: hablaba — yo/él/ella form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -aba. Used for habitual/repeated past actions.

quizQuestion:
{___} muy rápido cuando eras pequeño.
(You used to speak very fast when you were young.)
sentenceTranslation: You used to speak very fast when you were young.
explanation: hablabas — tú form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -abas.

quizQuestion:
Antes {___} todos los días por teléfono.
(Before, we used to talk every day by phone.)
sentenceTranslation: Before, we used to talk every day by phone.
explanation: hablábamos — nosotros form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -ábamos. Note the written accent.

quizQuestion:
Los estudiantes {___} cuando entré a la clase.
(The students were talking when I entered the classroom.)
sentenceTranslation: The students were talking when I entered the classroom.
explanation: hablaban — ellos form of hablar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: habl- + -aban. Describes an action that was ongoing when something else happened.

quizQuestion:
Antes {___} en una empresa grande.
(Before, I used to work at a large company.)
sentenceTranslation: Before, I used to work at a large company.
explanation: trabajaba — yo/él form of trabajar in the imperfect. Regular -ar: trabaj- + -aba.

quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} en París, iba mucho al museo.
(When I was living in Paris, I went to the museum a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: When I was living in Paris, I went to the museum a lot.
explanation: vivía — yo/él form of vivir in the imperfect. Regular -ir: viv- + -ía.

quizQuestion:
De niño {___} mucho chocolate.
(As a child I used to eat a lot of chocolate.)
sentenceTranslation: As a child I used to eat a lot of chocolate.
explanation: comía — yo/él form of comer in the imperfect. Regular -er: com- + -ía.

quizQuestion:
Antes, todos {___} en familia.
(Before, everyone used to eat together as a family.)
sentenceTranslation: Before, everyone used to eat together as a family.
explanation: comían — ellos form of comer in the imperfect. Regular -er: com- + -ían.

quizQuestion:
Cuando {___} niño, quería ser astronauta.
(When I was a child, I wanted to be an astronaut.)
sentenceTranslation: When I was a child, I wanted to be an astronaut.
explanation: era — yo/él/ella form of ser in the imperfect. Ser is irregular in the imperfect: er- + -a (yo/él/ella).

quizQuestion:
De niño {___} muy tímido.
(As a child you were very shy.)
sentenceTranslation: As a child you were very shy.
explanation: eras — tú form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: er- + -as.

quizQuestion:
{___} muy buenos amigos en la escuela.
(We were very good friends in school.)
sentenceTranslation: We were very good friends in school.
explanation: éramos — nosotros form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: éram- + -os. Note the written accent on the first é.

quizQuestion:
Mis abuelos {___} muy trabajadores.
(My grandparents were very hardworking.)
sentenceTranslation: My grandparents were very hardworking.
explanation: eran — ellos form of ser in the imperfect. Irregular: er- + -an.

quizQuestion:
De niño {___} al parque todos los domingos.
(As a child I used to go to the park every Sunday.)
sentenceTranslation: As a child I used to go to the park every Sunday.
explanation: iba — yo/él form of ir in the imperfect. Ir is irregular: ib- + -a (yo/él/ella).

quizQuestion:
¿Siempre {___} a la misma escuela?
(Did you always go to the same school?)
sentenceTranslation: Did you always go to the same school?
explanation: ibas — tú form of ir in the imperfect. Irregular: ib- + -as.

quizQuestion:
Antes {___} de vacaciones juntos cada verano.
(Before, we used to go on vacation together every summer.)
sentenceTranslation: Before, we used to go on vacation together every summer.
explanation: íbamos — nosotros form of ir in the imperfect. Irregular: íbam- + -os. Note the accent.

quizQuestion:
De pequeño {___} mucho la televisión.
(As a young child I used to watch TV a lot.)
sentenceTranslation: As a young child I used to watch TV a lot.
explanation: veía — yo/él form of ver in the imperfect. Ver is irregular: ve- + -ía (not the expected *vía).

quizQuestion:
¿Cuándo {___} a tus amigos normalmente?
(When did you normally see your friends?)
sentenceTranslation: When did you normally see your friends?
explanation: veías — tú form of ver in the imperfect. Irregular: ve- + -ías.

quizQuestion:
Mis padres {___} las noticias cada noche.
(My parents used to watch the news every night.)
sentenceTranslation: My parents used to watch the news every night.
explanation: veían — ellos form of ver in the imperfect. Irregular: ve- + -ían.

quizQuestion:
Cuando llegué a casa, él {___}.
(When I arrived home, he was sleeping.)
sentenceTranslation: When I arrived home, he was sleeping.
grammarPointLabel: Preterite vs. Imperfect contrast: background action
explanation: dormía — imperfect of dormir (to sleep). The preterite 'llegué' describes the completed action; the imperfect 'dormía' describes the ongoing background action at that moment.

quizQuestion:
{___} tarde cuando llegué.
(It was late when I arrived.)
sentenceTranslation: It was late when I arrived.
grammarPointLabel: Preterite vs. Imperfect contrast: past setting
explanation: era — imperfect of ser. 'Era tarde' sets the background context (imperfect); 'llegué' is the completed action that happened then (preterite).
